UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information and preferences within the Cyber-Sphere application, distinct from authentication credentials managed by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

displayName.description: The user's display name, used for personalization across the application.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface (e.g., 'en' for English, 'hi' for Hindi).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

lastActivityAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last recorded significant activity in the application.

LinkScanResult.title: LinkScanResult
LinkScanResult.description: Stores the results and details of a malicious link scan performed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LinkScanResult entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed this scan. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N LinkScanResult)

resultStatus.description: The overall outcome of the scan (e.g., 'safe', 'malicious', 'suspicious', 'error').

threatLevel.description: The perceived threat level if malicious or suspicious (e.g., 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical').

details.description: A detailed description or explanation of the scan result, including identified threats.

scanTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the scan was performed.

MessageScanResult.title: MessageScanResult
MessageScanResult.description: Stores the results and details of a fake message detection scan performed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MessageScanResult entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed this scan. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MessageScanResult)

messageContentPreview.description: A truncated or summarized version of the message content scanned, to avoid storing full sensitive data.

resultStatus.description: The overall outcome of the message scan (e.g., 'safe', 'phishing-detected', 'spam', 'suspicious').

threatLevel.description: The perceived threat level if malicious or suspicious (e.g., 'low', 'medium', 'high').

details.description: A detailed description or explanation of the scan result.

scanTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the message scan was performed.

DeepfakeScanResult.title: DeepfakeScanResult
DeepfakeScanResult.description: Stores the results and details of a deepfake image or video verification performed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DeepfakeScanResult entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed this scan. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DeepfakeScanResult)

mediaReference.description: A reference (e.g., URL or identifier) to the media that was scanned.

resultStatus.description: The overall outcome of the deepfake scan (e.g., 'authentic', 'deepfake-detected', 'suspicious', 'error').

confidenceScore.description: A numerical score indicating the confidence of the scan result (e.g., 0.0 to 1.0).

details.description: A detailed description or explanation of the scan result.

scanTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the deepfake scan was performed.

FileScanResult.title: FileScanResult
FileScanResult.description: Stores the results and details of a file malware scan performed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FileScanResult entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed this scan. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FileScanResult)

fileName.description: The original name of the file that was scanned.

fileIdentifier.description: A unique identifier for the scanned file, such as a content hash or internal storage ID.

resultStatus.description: The overall outcome of the file scan (e.g., 'clean', 'malware-detected', 'suspicious', 'error').

threatLevel.description: The perceived threat level if malware is detected or suspicious (e.g., 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical').

details.description: A detailed description or explanation of the scan result, including detected threats.

scanTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the file scan was performed.

QrCodeScanResult.title: QrCodeScanResult
QrCodeScanResult.description: Stores the results and details of a QR code security scan performed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QrCodeScanResult entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed this scan. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N QrCodeScanResult)

scannedContent.description: The content extracted from the scanned QR code (e.g., URL, text).

resultStatus.description: The overall outcome of the QR code scan (e.g., 'safe', 'malicious-link', 'suspicious', 'error').

threatLevel.description: The perceived threat level if malicious or suspicious (e.g., 'low', 'medium', 'high').

details.description: A detailed description or explanation of the scan result.

scanTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the QR code scan was performed.

ChatSession.title: ChatSession
ChatSession.description: Represents a single conversational session with the AI chatbot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this chat session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatSession)

startTime.description: Timestamp when the chat session started.

endTime.description: Timestamp when the chat session ended.

summary.description: A brief summary or topic of the conversation, potentially AI-generated.

lastMessageTimestamp.description: Timestamp of the last message sent or received in the session.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents an individual message sent or received within a ChatSession.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

chatSessionId.description: Reference to the ChatSession this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatSession 1:N ChatMessage)

content.description: The actual text content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent or received.

SecurityTip.title: SecurityTip
SecurityTip.description: Stores curated cybersecurity tips and advice for users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SecurityTip entity.

title.description: The title of the security tip.

content.description: The detailed content of the security tip.

category.description: The category of the tip (e.g., 'Phishing', 'Password Hygiene', 'Device Security').

severity.description: The importance or urgency of the tip (e.g., 'low', 'medium', 'high').

publishedAt.description: Timestamp when the security tip was first published.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the security tip was last updated.

NewsArticle.title: NewsArticle
NewsArticle.description: Stores aggregated or curated cybersecurity news articles for the dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NewsArticle entity.

title.description: The title of the news article.

summary.description: A brief summary or abstract of the news article.

externalUrl.description: The URL to the full external news article.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp when the news article was published.

source.description: The source or publisher of the news article.

UserLocation.title: UserLocation
UserLocation.description: Stores a user's geographical location for safety features, with explicit user consent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLocation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile whose location this is. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserLocation)

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the user's location.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the user's location.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this location was recorded.

CyberAttackReport.title: CyberAttackReport
CyberAttackReport.description: Stores information about reported cyber-attacks, potentially for display on maps.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CyberAttackReport entity.

reportType.description: The type of cyber-attack reported (e.g., 'DDoS', 'Ransomware', 'Phishing Campaign').

description.description: A brief description of the cyber-attack incident.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the reported attack's location.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the reported attack's location.

affectedArea.description: A generalized description of the affected geographical area (e.g., city, region, country).

reportedAt.description: Timestamp when the cyber-attack was reported or occurred.

source.description: The source of the report (e.g., 'user', 'OSINT feed', 'security partner').

status.description: The current status of the report (e.g., 'reported', 'verified', 'resolved').

EmergencyContact.title: EmergencyContact
EmergencyContact.description: Stores user-defined emergency contact information for the panic button feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmergencyContact entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this emergency contact. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N EmergencyContact)

name.description: The full name of the emergency contact.

relationship.description: The user's relationship to the emergency contact (e.g., 'parent', 'spouse', 'friend').

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the emergency contact.

email.description: The email address of the emergency contact.

PanicButtonLog.title: PanicButtonLog
PanicButtonLog.description: Logs activations of the emergency panic button by users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PanicButtonLog entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who activated the panic button. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PanicButtonLog)

activationTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the panic button was activated.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the user's location at the time of activation, if available.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the user's location at the time of activation, if available.

status.description: The status of the emergency response triggered (e.g., 'triggered', 'alert-sent', 'resolved', 'false-alarm').

notes.description: Additional notes or details about the panic button activation.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the owning user.
Stores malicious link scan results for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed the scan.

params.name: linkScanResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific link scan result.

definition.description: Stores fake message detection scan results for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed the scan.

params.name: messageScanResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific message scan result.

definition.description: Stores deepfake image/video verification results for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed the verification.

params.name: deepfakeScanResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific deepfake scan result.

definition.description: Stores file malware scan results for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed the file scan.

params.name: fileScanResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific file scan result.

definition.description: Stores QR code security scan results for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed the QR code scan.

params.name: qrCodeScanResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific QR code scan result.

definition.description: Stores individual AI chatbot sessions for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated the chat session.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chat session.

definition.description: Stores individual chat messages within a user's specific chat session. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'chatSessionId' for authorization independence and contextual queries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the chat session.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent chat session.

params.name: chatMessageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chat message.

definition.description: Stores a user's geographical location history for safety features. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user whose location is being tracked.

params.name: userLocationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific location record.

definition.description: Stores user-defined emergency contact information. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this emergency contact.

params.name: emergencyContactId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific emergency contact.

definition.description: Logs activations of the emergency panic button by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who activated the panic button.

params.name: panicButtonLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific panic button activation log.

definition.description: Stores global cybersecurity tips and advice. These documents are read-only for all authenticated users and managed by administrators.

params.name: securityTipId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific security tip.

definition.description: Stores global aggregated cybersecurity news articles. These documents are read-only for all authenticated users and managed by administrators.

params.name: newsArticleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific news article.

definition.description: Stores global reports of cyber-attacks for map display. These documents are read-only for all authenticated users and can be managed by administrators.

params.name: cyberAttackReportId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific cyber attack report.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to define users with administrative privileges. The existence of a document with a 'userId' as its ID indicates that the corresponding user is an administrator, following the 'Existence over Content' principle for DBAC.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of a user who is designated as an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Cyber-Sphere is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data is organized under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, leveraging path-based ownership. Each user-owned document within these subcollections (e.g., LinkScanResult, MessageScanResult, ChatSession) includes a denormalized `userId` field. This critical denormalization strategy eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document attributes for authorization, thereby achieving Authorization Independence. For instance, a `LinkScanResult` document at `/users/{userId}/linkScanResults/{linkScanResultId}` will contain `resource.data.userId`, allowing rules to simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` or `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path wildcard).

Publicly accessible or global data, such as `SecurityTip`, `NewsArticle`, and `CyberAttackReport`, is stored in dedicated top-level collections (`/securityTips`, `/newsArticles`, `/cyberAttackReports`). This structural segregation ensures that documents within a collection share the same security posture, simplifying rules significantly (e.g., read-only for all authenticated users).

Authorization Independence is further enforced by directly embedding the `userId` in all user-specific documents, even when the `userId` is already part of the document's path. This redundancy ensures that each document is self-contained regarding its ownership, simplifying data integrity checks and facilitating atomic operations without complex rule dependencies. For example, a `ChatMessage` at `/users/{userId}/chatSessions/{chatSessionId}/messages/{chatMessageId}` will include `userId` and `chatSessionId` as fields.

Query Against Permissions (QAPs) are robustly supported. For user-owned data, rules like `allow read: if request.auth.uid == userId;` automatically ensure that any `list` operation on `/users/{userId}/...` collections will only return documents owned by the authenticated user, as the query path itself includes the authenticated user's ID. For global collections (`/securityTips`, `/newsArticles`), where all authenticated users can read all documents, `list` operations naturally return all available documents, thus respecting the QAP principle without filtering. Admin roles are implemented using the 'Existence over Content' principle in a top-level `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection, allowing for direct, independent checks of administrative privileges without relying on custom claims or complex hierarchical lookups. This comprehensive approach results in a highly secure, performant, and easily auditable Firestore structure.
